flags.rewound_from_stage0_5_pivot: Case 6b (unsalvageable conjecture at flagship floor) + regression pattern (a) — both reviewer rounds reject on the same novelty-tier defect: the proposal as conjectured reduces to a useful subfield-tier finite-LP specialization with two mass caps, and the stronger five-family facet catalogue / strict-shrinkage frontier that would close the flagship gap was first only partially confirmed and then overclaimed via generic LP-duality language. The reviewer is explicit that flagship requires either a constructive exposed-basis enumeration (which Codex tried and failed to produce honestly) OR positioning against a named published open regime (which the conjecture's framing against Balke–Pearl / Richardson–Robins / symbolic discrete-bound methods does not deliver). Re-deriving the same conjecture at Stage 0 would burn budget on the same novelty-floor defect, not a fixable derivation step.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the average treatment effect from a binary instrument under bounded one-sided defier share and bounded never-taker share: closed-form polytopal identified set, sharpness certificate via boundary distributions, and exact limits recovering Imbens-Angrist LATE point identification when defier share equals zero and Manski-style outcome-support bounds when never-taker share equals one
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue artifacts are imbens_angrist_late_v1, manski_nonparametric_bounds_v1, the failed pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance proposal, and candidate pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2. The first two are limit anchors; the failed bounded-defiers proposal targeted a static four-cell envelope and was rejected for an omitted cell; the dynamic-IV proposal targets history-dependent compliance. None states the flagship kernel here: an open-set strict inequality between the true joint lower ATE endpoint L(P;c,delta,nu) and the strongest pairwise sensitivity diagnostic L^square(P;c,delta,nu).","published_axis":"The closest published comparator is Picchetti2026, which combines defier-share and c-dependence relaxations and derives IV identified sets and breakdown frontiers, but it has no independent never-taker-share coordinate. Noack2021 and Yap2022 vary defier-related sensitivity parameters without never-taker caps. BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024 addresses identifying power of generalized monotonicity restrictions, but not mass-cap restrictions that bind latent principal-stratum shares. The epsilon-gap is the non-rectangular three-coordinate ATE sign frontier: pairwise sharp sensitivity analyses can be strictly weaker than the joint frontier on an open set."}
seed_list: Prior work BalkePearl1997 establishes sharp unrestricted binary-IV ATE bounds. Our seed conjectures that adding simultaneous defier and never-taker caps creates a five-family affine facet envelope rather than a generic LP, because the two caps interact through the two untreated four-cell mixtures.

Prior proposal pid_late_bounded_defiers review said the old formula omitted the Z=1,D=0 cell. Our seed conjectures that the missing cell generates new joint-cap facets, because Z=1,D=0 and Z=0,D=0 constrain different hidden untreated mixtures.

Prior work LevisBonviniZengKeeleKennedy2025 treats Balke-Pearl covariate bounds as nonsmooth max/min functionals. Our seed conjectures that the two-cap IV envelope has a named active-facet map, because cap-binding facets separate from support-fill-in facets.

Prior work RichardsonRobins2010 studies binary-IV geometry with defier restrictions. Our seed conjectures that every two-cap endpoint has a constructive boundary distribution, because cap-binding and zero-cell faces require different primal witnesses.

Prior work ImbensAngrist1994 identifies LATE under zero defiers. Our seed conjectures that the zero-defier face of the ATE envelope recovers only the complier coordinate, not population ATE, because noncomplier counterfactual components remain free.

Prior work Manski1990 gives outcome-support bounds when counterfactual means are latent. Our seed conjectures that the pure never-taker face of the two-cap IV envelope equals the Manski support interval, because the instrument then reveals only Y(0).

Prior proposal pid_late_bounded_defiers claimed a point-identification phase transition. Our seed conjectures an iff singleton classification over caps and observed cell masses, because positive defier slack and never-taker degeneracy affect different coordinates.

Prior proposal pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 found pure-never-taker mixture-subtraction strictness. Our seed conjectures finite inequalities for when a static never-taker cap strictly tightens ATE bounds, because observed four-cell constraints can make the cap redundant or active.

Prior work Picchetti2026 combines defier-share and independence relaxations. Our seed conjectures a three-parameter sign frontier with never-taker caps added, because the never-taker cap changes both stratum masses and hidden counterfactual width.

Prior work BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024 asks which generalized monotonicity restrictions have identifying power. Our seed conjectures that mass caps can have identifying power exactly on nonredundant four-cell facets, because cap restrictions bind latent stratum shares rather than outcome ranks.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BalkePearl1997 establishes sharp unrestricted binary-IV ATE bounds. Our seed conjectures that adding simultaneous defier and never-taker caps creates a five-family affine facet envelope rather than a generic LP, because the two caps interact through the two untreated four-cell mixtures.

anchor_paper.author: Balke and Pearl
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
anchor_paper.claim: Gives tight nonparametric ATE bounds for binary outcome, treatment, and instrument using symbolic linear-programming logic, but not simultaneous defier and never-taker inequality caps.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern; not blocked because the proposed joint-cap facets can change the endpoint normal, not merely weaken monotonicity.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from BalkePearl1997 because adding linear cap inequalities is just another LP.
seed_details.rebuttal: Balke-Pearl gives unrestricted endpoint inequalities, while the epsilon-gap is the explicit projected two-cap facet catalogue and its active-family partition.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_late_bounded_defiers review said the old formula omitted the Z=1,D=0 cell. Our seed conjectures that the missing cell generates new joint-cap facets, because Z=1,D=0 and Z=0,D=0 constrain different hidden untreated mixtures.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal review
anchor_paper.claim: The formula only uses the Z=0,D=0 mixture and omits the Z=1,D=0 observed cell.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/sharpness-failure pattern; not blocked because it refutes a named prior CausalSmith proposal and promotes the obstruction to the full open four-cell class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is merely fixing a bug in an internal proposal, not a research question.
seed_details.rebuttal: The bug identifies the published-literature gap: simultaneous cap sharpness requires both untreated observed mixtures and yields joint-cap facets absent from the old kernel.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work LevisBonviniZengKeeleKennedy2025 treats Balke-Pearl covariate bounds as nonsmooth max/min functionals. Our seed conjectures that the two-cap IV envelope has a named active-facet map, because cap-binding facets separate from support-fill-in facets.

anchor_paper.author: Levis, Bonvini, Zeng, Keele, and Kennedy
anchor_paper.venue: Journal of the Royal Statistical Society Series B
anchor_paper.claim: Shows Balke-Pearl-style IV bounds with covariates are useful but nonsmooth max/min functionals, highlighting the need for explicit facet control.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition pattern; not blocked because it targets the nonsmooth active-facet mechanism created by two caps, not a CATE or covariate restatement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just inference packaging for known Balke-Pearl bounds.
seed_details.rebuttal: The active facets differ once defier and never-taker caps enter, so the inferential map is not available from unrestricted Balke-Pearl formulas.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work RichardsonRobins2010 studies binary-IV geometry with defier restrictions. Our seed conjectures that every two-cap endpoint has a constructive boundary distribution, because cap-binding and zero-cell faces require different primal witnesses.

anchor_paper.author: Richardson and Robins
anchor_paper.venue: Heuristics, Probability and Causality / University of Washington working paper
anchor_paper.claim: Analyzes the binary IV model geometry and bounds under restrictions including small or known defier proportions.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sharpness-certificate pattern; not blocked because the target is a named published IV geometry and requires nondegenerate boundary laws for the open two-cap class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from LP duality and needs no separate theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: LP duality proves existence of vertices, but Stage 0 needs explicit primal maps that handle cap-binding and zero-cell degeneracies.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work ImbensAngrist1994 identifies LATE under zero defiers. Our seed conjectures that the zero-defier face of the ATE envelope recovers only the complier coordinate, not population ATE, because noncomplier counterfactual components remain free.

anchor_paper.author: Imbens and Angrist
anchor_paper.claim: Identifies the LATE for units induced to change treatment status by the instrument under independence and monotonicity.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern; not blocked because it corrects a potential false ATE point-ID limit rather than extending LATE mechanically.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is textbook LATE, so it is not novel.
seed_details.rebuttal: The novelty is not LATE itself but the exact face equality and the warning that population ATE remains partially identified on the zero-defier face.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Manski1990 gives outcome-support bounds when counterfactual means are latent. Our seed conjectures that the pure never-taker face of the two-cap IV envelope equals the Manski support interval, because the instrument then reveals only Y(0).

anchor_paper.author: Manski
anchor_paper.claim: Provides nonparametric outcome-support bounds for treatment effects when counterfactual means are not point identified.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime-shift pattern; not blocked because it verifies a nontrivial face of the two-cap projection, not a new support-bound theorem.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Manski1990 because all units are untreated.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a standalone result yes; as a face-limit certificate for the two-cap IV projection it is a necessary sanity theorem tying the new envelope to known limits.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_late_bounded_defiers claimed a point-identification phase transition. Our seed conjectures an iff singleton classification over caps and observed cell masses, because positive defier slack and never-taker degeneracy affect different coordinates.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal review
anchor_paper.claim: The zero-threshold claim fails in the eta=0, pi_N>0 corner.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/phase-transition pattern; not blocked because it separates a refuted named phase-transition claim from the correct iff classification.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is cleanup of an internal mistake, not flagship novelty.
seed_details.rebuttal: The singleton classification would be a sharp corollary of the new endpoint formula and directly prevents false LATE-to-ATE limit claims.

seed_details.one_line: Prior proposal pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 found pure-never-taker mixture-subtraction strictness. Our seed conjectures finite inequalities for when a static never-taker cap strictly tightens ATE bounds, because observed four-cell constraints can make the cap redundant or active.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Strictness is witnessed by at least one endpoint pulled inward by the proper pure-never-taker set.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Assignment/target variation pattern; not blocked because it asks for strict/redundant inequalities in the static cap polytope, not a dynamic-to-static method transfer.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already in pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 because never-taker mixture subtraction was accepted there.
seed_details.rebuttal: The accepted dynamic proposal has a different target and does not characterize static binary-IV ATE cap redundancy after all four observed cells bind.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work Picchetti2026 combines defier-share and independence relaxations. Our seed conjectures a three-parameter sign frontier with never-taker caps added, because the never-taker cap changes both stratum masses and hidden counterfactual width.

anchor_paper.author: Picchetti
anchor_paper.claim: Combines defier-share relaxation with c-dependence relaxation of IV independence and derives identified sets and breakdown frontiers for LATE and ATE.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension pattern; blocked for current angle unless the never-taker cap changes sign-frontier geometry. Kept as lower-ranked future seed, not chosen.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an incremental third sensitivity parameter on Picchetti2026.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only survives if the never-taker cap introduces a new nonseparable frontier term; otherwise it should be dropped at pivot time.

seed_details.one_line: Prior work BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024 asks which generalized monotonicity restrictions have identifying power. Our seed conjectures that mass caps can have identifying power exactly on nonredundant four-cell facets, because cap restrictions bind latent stratum shares rather than outcome ranks.

anchor_paper.author: Bai, Huang, Moon, Shaikh, and Vytlacil
anchor_paper.claim: Clarifies which generalized monotonicity restrictions have identifying power for ATE.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism-decomposition pattern; not blocked if framed as facet-level identifying power rather than a generic monotonicity variant.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is already a generalized monotonicity identifying-power question.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed mechanism is cap-binding in principal-stratum masses, not generalized monotone outcome or treatment-response ordering.

proposed_from.literature_map: Manski 1990 AER supplies bounded-outcome support bounds; Imbens and Angrist 1994 Econometrica identifies LATE under independence and monotonicity; Angrist, Imbens, and Rubin 1996 JASA gives the principal-strata language; Balke and Pearl 1997 JASA gives sharp binary-IV ATE bounds by symbolic LP; Manski and Pepper 2000 Econometrica frames monotone IV tightening; Richardson and Robins 2010 analyze binary-IV geometry with defier-proportion restrictions; Mourifie and Wan 2017 RESTUD tests LATE independence and monotonicity; Swanson et al. 2018 JASA reviews binary-IV ATE partial identification and defier-proportion geometry; Noack 2021 and Yap 2022 develop defier-sensitivity bounds; Levis et al. 2025 JRSSB motivates explicit nonsmooth facet control for IV bounds; Picchetti 2026 combines defier-share and independence relaxations; Bai et al. 2024 studies identifying power of generalized monotonicity. Named tension: the literature has unrestricted Balke-Pearl IV bounds and exact monotone LATE faces, but no compact simultaneous defier-cap and never-taker-cap ATE envelope with boundary-distribution certificates.

{"status":"completed","mode":"pivot","version":1,"message":"Conjecture: the binary-IV ATE sign frontier is non-rectangular under joint c-dependence, defier caps, and never-taker caps.","literature_map":"Manski (1990, AER, Manski1990, https://EconPapers.repec.org/RePEc:aea:aecrev:v:80:y:1990:i:2:p:319-23) supplies the bounded-outcome support-bounds benchmark.
Imbens and Angrist (1994, Econometrica, ImbensAngrist1994, https://www.jstor.org/stable/2951620) identifies LATE under IV independence and monotonicity.
Angrist, Imbens, and Rubin (1996, JASA, AngristImbensRubin1996, https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.1996.10476902) gives the principal-strata language of compliers, never-takers, always-takers, and defiers.
Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA, BalkePearl1997, https://doi.org/10.1080/01621459.1997.10474074) gives sharp binary-IV ATE bounds through symbolic LP.
Noack (2021, arXiv, Noack2021, https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2106.06421) and Yap (2022, Princeton WP, Yap2022, http://arks.princeton.edu/ark:/88435/dsp015d86p341p) parameterize defier-related sensitivity without an independent never-taker cap.
Picchetti (2026, arXiv, Picchetti2026, https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2603.25529) combines defier-share and c-dependence relaxations and is the closest comparator for the new three-coordinate frontier.
Bai, Huang, Moon, Shaikh, and Vytlacil (2024, NBER, BaiHuangMoonShaikhVytlacil2024) clarify no-power regions for generalized monotonicity restrictions.

The closest failed prior is pid_late_bounded_defiers_one_sided_compliance, rejected because its formula omitted the Z=1,D=0 observed cell and did not solve the full four-cell mixture system.
The same failed prior flagged the eta=0 exact-Wald corner with never-takers as a flaw in an old phase-transition claim.
Accepted study artifacts imbens_angrist_late_v1 and manski_nonparametric_bounds_v1 provide limit anchors, not this three-coordinate frontier.
Candidate pid_dynamic_iv_compliance_v2 provides pure-never-taker mixture-subtraction language, but its target is dynamic and does not include Picchetti-style c-dependence.
